mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, consumer spending power is structurally impaired, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicate robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, driving positive equity returns as corporate revenue growth outpaces discount rate risks.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when CPIAUCSL rises above 0.5% month-over-month alongside RSAFS growth, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into short-term positive equity returns during early recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes noise with Rank IC near zero when tested in late-cycle or recession regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge with t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate wage income and consumer expenditure, creating a persistent positive drift in equity markets over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with forward returns with Rank IC below 0.02, or positive return frequency falls below 50% during periods of sustained labor market expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of premature monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for explicit Federal Reserve forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding real GDP indicate synchronized residential and broad economic recovery, amplifying corporate earnings revisions and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive significance with p-value above 0.10, or the signal produces negative returns when GDPC1 growth decouples from HOUST trends in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, declining unemployment rates signal peak economic activity and maximum corporate profitability, generating positive forward returns before recessionary risks materialize.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive returns when tested in early-cycle or recession regimes, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in out-of-sample late-cycle windows over rolling 2-year periods.
